children.heading: .—Judicial branch
children.label: 1 .—Judicial branch
children.text: Subject to this section, the Architect of the Capitol shall make available to the judicial branch of the Federal Government all space in the Thurgood Marshall Federal Judiciary Building and other improvements constructed under this chapter. The space shall be made available on a reimbursable basis and substantially in accordance with the report referred to in section 3(b)(1) of the Judiciary Office Building Development Act (, ).Public Law 100–480102 Stat. 2330

children.heading: .—Other federal governmental entities
children.label: 2 .—Other federal governmental entities
children.text: The Architect may make available to federal governmental entities which are not part of the judicial branch and which are not staff of Members of Congress or congressional committees any space in the Building and other improvements that the Chief Justice decides is not needed by the judicial branch. The space shall be made available on a reimbursable basis.

children.heading: .—Other persons
children.label: 3 .—Other persons
children.text: If any space remains, the Architect may sublease it pursuant to subsection (e), under the direction of the Commission for the Judiciary Office Building, to any person.

children.heading: .—Space for Judicial Branch and Other Federal Governmental Entities
children.label: b .—Space for Judicial Branch and Other Federal Governmental Entities

children.text: terms and conditions necessary to carry out the objectives of this chapter; and

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of the judicial branch, the Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any federal governmental entity not a part of the judicial branch, the entity.

children.heading: .—Assignment of space within judicial branch
children.label: 1 .—Assignment of space within judicial branch
children.text: The Director may assign space made available to the judicial branch under subsection (a)(1) among offices of the judicial branch as the Director considers appropriate.

children.heading: .—Vacating occupied space
children.label: 2 .—Vacating occupied space
children.text: When the Chief Justice notifies the Architect that the judicial branch requires additional space in the Building and other improvements, the Architect shall accommodate those requirements within 90 days after the date of the notification, except that if the space was made available to the Administrator of General Services, it shall be vacated expeditiously by not later than a date the Chief Justice and the Administrator agree on.

children.heading: .—Unoccupied space
children.label: 3 .—Unoccupied space
children.text: The Chief Justice has the right of first refusal to use unoccupied space in the Building to meet the needs of the judicial branch.

children.heading: .—Authority to lease
children.label: 1 .—Authority to lease
children.text: Subject to approval by the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate, the House Office Building Commission, and the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate, the Architect may lease and occupy not more than 75,000 square feet of space in the Building.

children.label: A
children.text: to the Architect to carry out this subsection, including amounts for acquiring and installing furniture and furnishings; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the Sergeant at Arms of the Senate to plan for, acquire, and install telecommunications equipment and services for the Architect with respect to space leased under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Rental rate
children.label: 1 .—Rental rate
children.text: Space subleased by the Architect under subsection (a)(3) is subject to reimbursement at a rate which is comparable to prevailing rental rates for similar facilities in the area but not less than the rate established under plus an amount the Architect and the person subleasing the space agree is necessary to pay each year for the cost of administering the Building (including the cost of operation, maintenance, rehabilitation, security, and structural, mechanical, and domestic care) that is attributable to the space.section 6504(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 2 .—Limitation
children.text: A sublease under subsection (a)(3) must be compatible with the dignity and functions of the judicial branch offices housed in the Building and must not unduly interfere with the activities and operations of the judicial branch agencies housed in the Building. Sections 5104(c) and 5108 of this title do not apply to any space in the Building and other improvements subleased to a non-Government tenant under subsection (a)(3).

children.heading: .—Collection of rent
children.label: 3 .—Collection of rent
children.text: The Architect shall collect rent for space subleased under subsection (a)(3).

children.heading: .—Deposit of Rent and Reimbursements
children.label: f .—Deposit of Rent and Reimbursements
children.text: Amounts received under subsection (a)(3) (including lease payments and reimbursements) shall be deposited in the account described in .section 6507 of this title
